reflection:
5am, repeating the speech in your sleep, feverish before you'd even said a word, heart pounding at 6 out of 10 on waking. that was yesterday morning. and then you got up and did the thing anyway.

here's what the week actually shows. saturday your anxiety was a 7, you said you'd gotten all the worries in your head possible. sunday you met everyone and it dropped to a 3, mood up to 8. then speech morning it spiked back to 6. every single time, the dread was bigger than the doing. that's the pattern, and it's worth seeing clearly. the speech was never the monster. the waiting was.

you delivered it. you said what you wanted to say, you opened up a little. those are your own words from last night. and then in the same breath you're asking where your balls are and deciding people didn't like it. that last part isn't a fact, it's the adrenaline still draining out of you. you don't actually have data on how it landed. you have a feeling, and the feeling is just leftover fear looking for somewhere to go.

one thing today. don't replay the speech. it's done, it's behind you, and you've got a week with the team still in front of you. be in that, not in the tape.

you did the scary thing. that's the whole game.
opener: you woke at 5 terrified and gave the speech anyway. that's the win.
focus_for_today: don't replay the speech. it's done. be with the team today, not in the tape.
